content: If $$R$$ is a relation from a finite set A having $$m$$ elements to finite set $$B$$ having $$n$$ elements, then the number of relation from $$A$$ to $$B$$ is
explanation:
We know that each subset of $$A \times B$$ will form a relation.

Therefore, number of relations from $$A$$ to $$B$$ is

$$\because n(A \times B)=n(A) \times n(B)=m n$$

$$\therefore$$ Number of subset of $$A \times B=2^{m n}$$

Hence, number of relation from $$A$$ to $$B=2^{m n}$$

content: The cartesian product $$A \times A$$ has 9 elements among which are found $$(-1,0)$$ and $$(0,1)$$, then set $$A$$ is equal to
explanation:
Given, $$\quad n(A \times A)=9 \Rightarrow n(A) \times n(A)=9$$ which is possible when, $$n(A)=3$$.

i.e., set $$A$$ contain only three elements.

i.e., $$A=\{1,-1,0\}$$

So, $$A \times A$$ contain $$(-1,0)$$ and $$(0,1)$$.

content: If $$S=\{(a, b): b=|a-1|, a \in Z$$ and $$|a|<3\}$$, where $$Z$$ denotes the set of integers. Then, the range set of $$S$$ is
explanation:
Given, $$S=\{(a, b): b=|a-1|, a \in z$$ and $$|a|<3\}$$

$$z \rightarrow$$ set of integers.

$$\therefore S=\{(-2,3),(-1,2),(0,1),(1,0),(2,1)\}$$

$$\therefore$$ Range of $$\operatorname{set}(S)=\{0,1,2,3\}$$

$$(\because|a|<3 \Rightarrow-3 < a < 3)$$
